prophecies.q: 帝曰：天下之事若何？先生知碗中为何物？
prophecies.a:
半似日兮半似月，曾被金龙咬一缺。依臣之见，碗中乃烧饼是也。

白话文翻译：这东西半像太阳半像月亮，曾被金龙（皇帝）咬掉了一块。依我之见，碗里扣着的是一块烧饼。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What of the affairs of the realm? Does the master know what is in the bowl?
prophecies.a_en:
Half like the sun, half like the moon, once bitten by the golden dragon — a piece is missing. In your humble servant's view, it is a sesame flatbread beneath the bowl.

Vernacular translation: This thing is half like the sun and half like the moon, with a piece bitten off by the golden dragon (the Emperor). In my judgment, the object hidden beneath the bowl is a sesame flatbread.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此为烧饼歌开篇引子，刘伯温准确猜中朱元璋碗中所扣之物为咬了一口的烧饼，以此展开预言对话。
prophecies.verdict_en: This is the prologue of the Shaobing Song. Liu Bowen correctly guessed that the object hidden under the emperor's bowl was a half-eaten sesame flatbread, setting the stage for the prophetic dialogue.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：我朝大明天下，国运如何？
prophecies.a:
我朝大明一统世界，南方终灭北方终。嫡裔太子是嫡裔，文星高拱日防西。

白话文翻译：大明王朝统一天下，南方平定后北方也最终统一。嫡系太子本是正统继承人，但需提防西方方位有文曲星高照之人。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: Our Great Ming rules all under heaven — what of its fate?
prophecies.a_en:
Our Great Ming unifies the world; the south is subdued, the north ultimately follows. The legitimate heir is indeed the rightful prince, yet beware: a literary star rises high in the west.

Vernacular translation: The Ming dynasty unifies all under heaven — the south pacified and the north ultimately consolidated. The legitimate crown prince is the rightful heir, but one must beware of a star of literary brilliance rising in the western direction.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「嫡裔太子」指建文帝朱允炆为太子朱标嫡孙。「文星高拱日防西」暗示需提防的威胁来自西方——燕王朱棣封地在北平（相对南京偏西北），后发动靖难之役夺位。
prophecies.verdict_en: The 'legitimate heir' refers to the Jianwen Emperor Zhu Yunwen, grandson of Crown Prince Zhu Biao. 'A literary star rises high — beware the west' hints at the threat from Prince of Yan Zhu Di, whose fief was in Beiping (northwest of Nanjing), who later launched the Jingnan Campaign to seize the throne.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：此人是谁？有何变故？
prophecies.a:
此城御驾尽亲征，一院山河永乐平。秃顶人来文墨苑，英雄一半尽还乡。

白话文翻译：此城中御驾亲征出发，一片山河在永乐年间趋于太平。秃顶之人来到文墨翰林之苑，英雄们大半都回乡退隐了。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: Who is this person? What upheaval will there be?
prophecies.a_en:
From this city the imperial chariot marches forth in person; the realm finds peace under 'Eternal Joy.' A bald-headed man enters the literary court; half the heroes return to their homelands.

Vernacular translation: From this city the emperor personally leads his troops on campaign; the realm is pacified during the Yongle reign. A bald-headed man enters the court of scholars; half the heroes retire and return home.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「永乐平」直指永乐年号（1402-1424），朱棣即位后年号永乐。「秃顶人」暗指辅佐朱棣的僧人道衍（姚广孝），他是靖难之役的关键谋士。「英雄一半尽还乡」指朱棣登基后对建文旧臣的清洗。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Eternal Joy' directly names the Yongle era (1402-1424) of Emperor Zhu Di. 'A bald-headed man' alludes to the monk Dao Yan (Yao Guangxiao), the key strategist behind the Jingnan Campaign. 'Half the heroes return home' refers to Zhu Di's purge of Jianwen-era officials after seizing power.

prophecies.a:
北方胡虏残生命，御驾亲征得太平。失算功臣不敢谏，旧灵遗嘱末勤行。

白话文翻译：北方胡虏残害生灵，皇帝御驾亲征才得太平。失算的功臣不敢进谏，先帝的遗嘱也未能勤勉执行。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What happens after the Yongle era?
prophecies.a_en:
Northern barbarians ravage lives; the emperor marches forth in person to restore peace. Ministers who miscalculated dare not remonstrate; the old testament's instructions go unheeded.

Vernacular translation: Northern barbarians ravage the people; only the emperor's personal campaign restores peace. Officials who misjudged dare not speak up; the late emperor's dying wishes are not diligently followed.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「北方胡虏」指瓦剌（蒙古部落）入侵。「御驾亲征」指明英宗朱祁镇在太监王振怂恿下御驾亲征，于1449年土木堡之变中被瓦剌俘虏。「失算功臣不敢谏」指王振专权，群臣不敢反对亲征决定。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Northern barbarians' refers to the Oirat (Mongol) invasion. 'Imperial campaign' refers to Emperor Yingzong's ill-fated personal campaign at the urging of eunuch Wang Zhen, which led to his capture at the Tumu Crisis of 1449. 'Ministers who miscalculated dare not remonstrate' refers to Wang Zhen's autocratic power and officials' inability to oppose the campaign.

prophecies.a:
大将南征胆气豪，腰横秋水雁翎刀。风吹鼍鼓山河动，电闪旌旗日月高。

白话文翻译：大将南征时胆气豪迈，腰间横挂秋水般锋利的雁翎刀。风吹战鼓山河为之震动，旌旗在电闪中高扬如日月。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What follows after the Tumu Crisis?
prophecies.a_en:
The great general marches south with fearless valor, a gleaming saber slung at his waist. Wind-beaten drums shake mountains and rivers; lightning-lit banners fly as high as sun and moon.

Vernacular translation: The great general campaigns with bold courage, wearing a gleaming saber at his waist. Wind-driven war drums shake the mountains and rivers; banners flash in the lightning, soaring high as the sun and moon.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段暗指于谦在土木堡之变后力挽狂澜。英宗被俘后，于谦拥立景泰帝，组织北京保卫战击退瓦剌，稳定了明朝局势。于谦后被英宗复辟后冤杀。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage alludes to Yu Qian's heroic defense after the Tumu Crisis. After Emperor Yingzong's capture, Yu Qian installed the Jingtai Emperor and organized the defense of Beijing, repelling the Oirat forces and stabilizing the Ming dynasty. Yu Qian was later unjustly executed when Yingzong was restored to power.

prophecies.a:
天下饥寒有怪异，栋梁龙德乘婴儿。禁宫阔大任横走，长大金龙太平时。

白话文翻译：天下饥寒交迫之时有怪异之事，有龙德之人趁幼帝年幼时把持朝政。在广阔的禁宫中任意横行，待金龙长大成人才重归太平。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: Is the realm at peace thereafter?
prophecies.a_en:
When the realm suffers cold and hunger, strange things occur; a man of dragon virtue rides upon the infant. He roams freely through the vast Forbidden City; only when the golden dragon grows up does peace return.

Vernacular translation: When famine and cold grip the land, strange events unfold — a man of dragon virtue exploits the young emperor's infancy. He roams freely through the vast Forbidden City; peace returns only when the golden dragon comes of age.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段暗指明朝中期宦官专权。「乘婴儿」指太监趁幼帝年幼把持朝政，如正德年间刘瑾专权。「长大金龙太平时」指嘉靖帝即位后整顿朝纲。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage alludes to eunuch dominance in mid-Ming politics. 'Rides upon the infant' refers to eunuchs seizing power during young emperors' reigns, such as Liu Jin's autocracy during the Zhengde era. 'When the golden dragon grows up, peace returns' refers to the Jiajing Emperor's ascension and restoration of order.

prophecies.a:
老拙那知天数短，思量干戈为太平。一个千年如意珠，四夷各自都消灭。

白话文翻译：老朽我哪知天数如此之短，只想通过征战来实现太平。一颗千年如意宝珠，四方蛮夷各自都被消灭。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What comes after?
prophecies.a_en:
This old fool did not know heaven's term was so short; warfare was undertaken for the sake of peace. A thousand-year wish-fulfilling pearl; the barbarians on all four sides are each destroyed.

Vernacular translation: This old man did not realize how short heaven's allotted time was; military campaigns were waged to achieve lasting peace. A wish-fulfilling pearl lasting a thousand years; enemies on all four borders are each vanquished.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段暗指嘉靖至万历年间（1521-1620），明朝抗击倭寇、平定边患。戚继光等名将荡平倭寇，「四夷各自都消灭」指东南倭患和北方蒙古威胁的先后平定。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage alludes to the Jiajing-Wanli period (1521-1620), when the Ming fought Japanese pirates and border threats. Generals like Qi Jiguang defeated the Japanese raiders; 'barbarians on all sides destroyed' refers to the pacification of both the southeastern pirate menace and northern Mongol threats.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：朕之天下，后世子孙如何？
prophecies.a:
串去中直传天下，却是春禾换日头。猴子沐盆鸡逐鹿，犯着猪儿虑。

白话文翻译：「串」字去掉中间竖笔成为「患」，暗示天下将有忧患。春天的禾苗换了日头（朝代更替），属猴之人在沐盆中（弄权），属鸡之人逐鹿中原，犯到了属猪之人的忧虑。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What of my descendants who shall rule after me?
prophecies.a_en:
Remove the middle stroke from 'chuan' and pass the realm on; spring grain changes the sun overhead. The monkey bathes in a basin while the rooster chases the deer; trouble comes when the pig is encountered.

Vernacular translation: Removing the center stroke from 'chuan' (串) yields 'huan' (患, worry) — the realm shall face troubles. The spring crop sees a new sun (dynastic change); the monkey rules in the basin, the rooster chases the deer, and trouble comes when the pig is met.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段暗指明朝万历末年至天启年间的朝政混乱。「猴子沐盆」暗指万历帝属猴、「鸡逐鹿」指天启帝属鸡年间魏忠贤专权。万历至崇祯年间政局动荡，最终导致明朝覆亡。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage alludes to late-Ming political chaos from the Wanli to Tianqi periods. 'Monkey in the basin' hints at the Wanli Emperor (born in a Monkey year); 'rooster chases the deer' refers to the Tianqi era when eunuch Wei Zhongxian seized power. Political turmoil from Wanli to Chongzhen ultimately led to the Ming's fall.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：明朝灭亡之兆如何？
prophecies.a:
万子万孙层迭层，祖宗山上贝衣行。公侯不复朝金阙，十八孩儿难上难。

白话文翻译：万代子孙层层叠叠（万历的子孙），在祖宗的基业上穿着贝衣行走（衰败之象）。公侯们不再朝拜金銮殿，十八个孩子处境极其艰难。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What are the signs of the Ming's fall?
prophecies.a_en:
Ten thousand sons and grandsons layer upon layer; upon the ancestral mountain they walk in shell-cloth. Dukes and lords no longer attend the golden court; eighteen children face difficulties upon difficulties.

Vernacular translation: Countless descendants pile up in layers; upon the ancestral foundation they walk in humble garb. Nobles no longer attend the golden court; the eighteen children face hardship upon hardship.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「十八孩儿」合为「李」字（十八子），暗指李自成。1644年李自成攻破北京，崇祯帝自缢，明朝灭亡。「公侯不复朝金阙」指明末群臣离散，无人再朝见皇帝。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Eighteen children' combines to form the character 'Li' (李, ten + eight + child), alluding to Li Zicheng. In 1644, Li Zicheng captured Beijing, the Chongzhen Emperor hanged himself, and the Ming dynasty fell. 'Lords no longer attend the golden court' refers to the scattering of officials at the dynasty's end.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：十八孩儿何事？此后天下归谁？
prophecies.a:
木下一了头，目上一刀一戊丁。天下重文不重武，英雄豪杰总无春。

白话文翻译：「木下一了头」为「李」字的变体拆法，「目上一刀」为「自」字上加刀为「首」，「一戊丁」合为「成」，合起来就是「李自成」。天下重文不重武，英雄豪杰们都没有好结局。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What of the eighteen children? Who shall rule the realm next?
prophecies.a_en:
Beneath the tree a stroke completes the head; above the eye, a blade and 'wu-ding.' The realm values writing over warfare; heroes and champions find no spring.

Vernacular translation: Decomposing the characters yields 'Li Zi Cheng' (李自成). The realm values civil over military arts; heroes and champions all meet unhappy ends.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段通过拆字法明确指出「李自成」三字。李自成于1644年建立大顺政权，攻破北京推翻明朝，但仅在位42天即被清军和吴三桂联军击败，后败亡于湖北。「英雄豪杰总无春」指李自成的大顺政权短命。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage uses character decomposition to spell out 'Li Zicheng' (李自成). Li Zicheng established the Shun dynasty in 1644, captured Beijing and overthrew the Ming, but ruled for only 42 days before being defeated by the combined forces of the Qing army and Wu Sangui. 'Heroes find no spring' refers to the brevity of his reign.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：李自成之后天下归谁？
prophecies.a:
手执钢刀九十九，杀尽胡人方罢休。炮响火烟迷去路，天灰地变是春秋。

白话文翻译：手持钢刀九十九次征战，杀尽胡人才肯罢手。炮火硝烟迷蒙了前路，天昏地暗之间朝代更替。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: After Li Zicheng, who rules the realm?
prophecies.a_en:
Wielding a steel blade ninety-nine times, they slaughter until no barbarians remain. Cannon smoke obscures the road ahead; heaven dims and earth transforms — a new era dawns.

Vernacular translation: Steel blades are wielded ninety-nine times in war, fighting until every barbarian is vanquished. Cannon smoke obscures the way forward; in the dimming of heaven and upheaval of earth, dynasties change.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段暗指吴三桂引清兵入关。「手执钢刀九十九」，九十九暗合「白」字（百少一），指满族的白山黑水之地。清军入关后与南明和农民军反复征战，最终统一中国。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage alludes to Wu Sangui inviting the Qing (Manchu) army through Shanhai Pass. 'Ninety-nine' hints at the character 'bai' (白, hundred minus one), referencing the Manchu homeland of 'white mountains and black waters.' The Qing army entered China in 1644 and waged prolonged campaigns against the Southern Ming and peasant armies before unifying the country.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：此后胡人天下如何？
prophecies.a:
水浸月宫主上立，禾米一木并将去。二十年来遍地烟，一方冠冕天中坐。

白话文翻译：「水浸月宫」为「清」字（水+月+主），「禾米一木」合为「精」。清朝建立后主上临朝，二十年间遍地战火硝烟，最终一方冠冕稳坐天下。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: How shall the realm fare under barbarian rule?
prophecies.a_en:
Water floods the moon palace — the ruler stands; grain and tree together depart. For twenty years smoke covers the land; one crowned head sits in the center of heaven.

Vernacular translation: 'Water flooding the moon palace' encodes the character 'Qing' (清). After the Qing is established and the ruler ascends, twenty years of warfare fill the land with smoke before one crowned head finally sits secure upon the throne.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 通过拆字法暗示「清」朝国号。清军入关后，经历约二十年战争（1644-1662南明抗清），最终平定三藩、收复台湾，统一全国。「一方冠冕天中坐」指清朝皇帝稳坐江山。
prophecies.verdict_en: Character decomposition encodes the dynasty name 'Qing' (清). After entering China, the Qing spent roughly twenty years fighting (1644-1662 against the Southern Ming) before pacifying the Three Feudatories and recovering Taiwan to unify the country. 'One crowned head sits in heaven's center' indicates the Qing emperor's secure rule.

prophecies.a:
一二三四五六七，半边山河在手上。戴鸡蒙犬同起舞，八方混战各争强。

白话文翻译：一二三四五六七（缺八，暗指「王」字少一横为「亡」），半壁江山在手中。鸡犬之辈一同作乱起舞，八方混战群雄各自争强。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What is the fate of the Qing dynasty?
prophecies.a_en:
One, two, three, four, five, six, seven — half the realm rests in their hands. Roosters and dogs rise to dance together; eight directions descend into war, each fighting for dominance.

Vernacular translation: One through seven (missing 'eight,' hinting at ruin) — half the realm is in their grasp. Petty men of roosters and dogs rise together to make trouble; on all eight sides, warlords fight for supremacy.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「一二三四五六七」缺「八」，暗喻清朝终将灭亡。「半边山河」指太平天国占据半壁江山（1851-1864）。「鸡犬同起舞」指各路人马乘乱而起。「八方混战」指清末各地起义不断。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'One through seven' missing 'eight' implies the dynasty's eventual doom. 'Half the realm' refers to the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom occupying half of China (1851-1864). 'Roosters and dogs dance together' alludes to various factions rising in the chaos. 'Eight directions at war' refers to the constant uprisings across late-Qing China.

prophecies.a:
头生角拂尘来扫，大海流水各相通。一人为大世界福，龙蛇走马无归路。

白话文翻译：头上生角（暗喻「总」字）拿着拂尘来扫除旧制度，大海中的水路各自相通。一人成为大人物造福世界，龙蛇走马之年（辛亥年属蛇之后）天下大变无可回头。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What becomes of China after this?
prophecies.a_en:
A horned head sweeps with a duster; the great ocean's waters flow and connect. One man becomes the world's great blessing; dragons and snakes gallop forth — there is no road back.

Vernacular translation: A horned head (hinting at the concept of 'president') sweeps away the old order with a duster; ocean routes connect across the world. One great man brings blessing to the world; in the year of dragons and snakes, a sweeping transformation from which there is no return.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此段暗指辛亥革命和中华民国建立。「头生角」暗指「总统」（角为总字偏旁变化）。「大海流水各相通」指近代中国开放通商。辛亥革命（1911年，辛亥属猪）推翻清朝，建立民国，「无归路」指帝制不可复辟。
prophecies.verdict_en: This passage alludes to the Xinhai Revolution and the founding of the Republic of China. 'Horned head' hints at 'president.' 'Ocean waters flow and connect' refers to modern China's opening to trade. The Xinhai Revolution (1911) overthrew the Qing dynasty and established the Republic; 'no road back' means the imperial system could not be restored.

prophecies.a:
日头落水波涛尽，江山到此是非多。大刀阔斧改不了，红花满地众人和。

白话文翻译：太阳落入水中波涛平息（日本投降），此后的江山是非争端颇多。大刀阔斧也改变不了局面，红花铺满大地众人和睦。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What follows after the Republic?
prophecies.a_en:
The sun falls into the water and waves subside; from here the realm sees much dispute. Grand reforms cannot change the course; red flowers cover the land and the people unite.

Vernacular translation: The sun sinks into the water and the waves cease (Japan's surrender); the realm is then full of disputes and conflicts. Sweeping reforms cannot alter the course; red flowers blanket the land as the people unite in harmony.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「日头落水」暗指日本战败投降（1945年，「日」落入水中）。「江山是非多」指抗战后国共内战。「红花满地众人和」暗指1949年新中国成立，红色政权统一大陆，红旗遍布。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Sun falls into water' alludes to Japan's defeat and surrender (1945 — 'sun' sinks into water). 'Much dispute in the realm' refers to the Chinese Civil War after WWII. 'Red flowers cover the land, people unite' alludes to the founding of the People's Republic of China in 1949, with the red flag covering the mainland.

prophecies.q: 帝曰：红花之后天下何如？
prophecies.a:
莺啼凤舞出新朝，天下同心共一巢。万古千秋留此说，白云苍狗变天涯。

白话文翻译：黄莺啼鸣凤凰起舞迎来新朝代，天下同心同德共建一个家园。千秋万代留下这番话语，世事如白云苍狗变幻无常直到天涯。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: What of the realm after the red flowers?
prophecies.a_en:
Orioles sing and phoenixes dance as a new dynasty emerges; all under heaven share one heart and one nest. Through ten thousand ages this prophecy remains; white clouds become grey dogs — change reaches the ends of the earth.

Vernacular translation: Orioles sing and phoenixes dance to welcome a new era; all the people unite as one family. This prophecy shall endure through countless ages; yet the world is ever-changing, like white clouds that shift into grey dogs, transforming endlessly across the horizon.

prophecies.a:
前后相续不断绝，万万年来有圣贤。天机不可多泄漏，只待有缘人自验。

白话文翻译：前后相续绵延不绝，万万年来都会有圣贤出世。天机不可过多泄露，只等有缘之人自行验证。
prophecies.q_en: The Emperor asked: Has the master any final words?
prophecies.a_en:
Before and after, the succession is unbroken; through the ages, sages shall always appear. Heaven's secrets must not be revealed too freely; let those with destiny verify them in their own time.

Vernacular translation: The succession continues without end; across countless ages, sages and worthies will always arise. The secrets of heaven must not be disclosed too freely; let those who are destined discover and verify them on their own.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此为烧饼歌结尾，刘伯温以「天机不可多泄漏」收束全篇，与推背图「不如推背去归休」异曲同工，表达预言者适可而止、不可过度泄露天机的态度。
prophecies.verdict_en: This is the closing of the Shaobing Song. Liu Bowen ends with 'heaven's secrets must not be revealed too freely,' echoing the Tui Bei Tu's 'better to push the back and retire.' Both express the prophet's stance of knowing when to stop and not revealing too much of heaven's plan.
